title: Time-Dependent Dynamical Dimensional Transmutation in the $SU(2)$ Gross-Neveu Model with Time-Dependent Interaction Strength
abstract: In this work we consider the time-dependent $SU(2)$ Gross-Neveu model, which is a quantum field theory of fermions which interact with each other through spin exchange interaction with time-dependent coupling strength $g(t)$. Using the recently formulated generalized Bethe ansatz framework, we show that the system is integrable provided the time-dependent coupling strength is such that its trajectories in time are exactly same as that of the renormalization group (RG) flow equations corresponding to the static model, where time `$t$' of the time-dependent model is identified with the logarithm of the cutoff `$\ln Λ$' of the static model. In the scaling regime $Λ\rightarrow\infty$, the above relation between time and the logarithm of the cutoff provides a characteristic time scale $t_0$. We analyze the exact time-dependent wavefunction in the case of coupling strength decreasing with time and show that in the adiabatic regime, which corresponds to $t\sim t_0$ for drive rate $α_0=1$, the system exhibits a time-dependent dynamical dimensional transmutation where a time dependent mass gap is generated, which at time $t=t_0+Δt$ is given by $m(Δt)=m_0 e^{-πα_0Δt}$, where $m_0=Λe^{-πα_0 t_0}$. Comparing this with the mass gap of the static model, we identify the adiabatic regime of the time-dependent model with the scaling regime of the static model. In the case of very large time scales $t\gg t_0$ for drive rate $α_0$ or for very fast drive rates $α$ such that $αt \gg α_0t_0$, for any $t<L$, we argue that the system is asymptotically free and approaches the $SU(2)_1$ Wess-Zumino-Novikov-Witten (WZNW) model, which corresponds to the UV fixed point of the $SU(2)$ Gross-Neveu model. Hence we establish that progression of time in the time-dependent model is equivalent to RG flow in the corresponding static model.
summary: This paper shows that a time-dependent SU(2) Gross-Neveu model can be solved exactly if its coupling strength evolves according to the Renormalization Group flow. It demonstrates that time evolution in this model mimics the change in energy scale, leading to a dynamically generated, time-dependent mass gap.
main_problem: Investigating whether the connection between time-dependent integrability and Renormalization Group (RG) flow manifests at a deeper physical level in the SU(2) Gross-Neveu model.
main_result: The study establishes that the progression of time in the time-dependent model is equivalent to the RG flow in the static model, demonstrating a dynamical dimensional transmutation where a time-dependent mass gap is generated.
method: The author uses the generalized Bethe ansatz framework, solving quantum Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov (qKZ) equations and evaluating the Yang-Yang action via a saddle-point approximation.
model_or_system: The SU(2) Gross-Neveu model, a quantum field theory of interacting fermions with a time-dependent spin-exchange interaction strength g(t).
key_observables: Time-dependent mass gap m(t), instantaneous energy E(t), and the Yang-Yang action.
important_parameters: Drive rate alpha, characteristic time scale t0, coupling strength g(t), and the UV fixed point (SU(2)1 WZNW model).
main_assumptions: The integrability of the system is maintained by assuming the time-dependent coupling strength follows a specific 'RG protocol' trajectory.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the RG protocol, comparing the adiabatic regime (mass gap generation) with the fast-driving regime (approach to the UV fixed point).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the time-dependent model, constructs an exact N-particle wavefunction using the generalized Bethe ansatz, reduces the problem to qKZ equations, and analyzes the adiabatic and fast-driving regimes using the Yang-Yang action and saddle-point methods.
why_interesting: It provides a profound theoretical link between the temporal evolution of a driven many-body system and the energy-scale evolution of the Renormalization Group, which is highly relevant for understanding non-equilibrium dynamics in correlated systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Figure depicts adiabatic regime and fast driving regime of the integrability preserving RG protocol. In the RG protocol, the time-dependent coupling strengths are cho- sen such that their trajectories in time are same as that of the RG trajectories of the corresponding static model. In the adiabatic regime, which identifies with the scaling regime of the static SU(2) Gross-Neveu model, the system dynamically generates a time-dependent mass gap m(t). In the fast driv- ing regime, the coupling strength decreases at a much faster rate and the system becomes asymptotically free reaching the UV fixed point, which is the SU(2)1 WZNW model.
